dagbani: A noli yi zɔri vi ni bɔhibu di wumd'a mi.
english: If your mouth runs from shame of asking it will hear you (suffer).
usage: Being too shy to ask leads to problems

dagbani: A yi bi ʒi ma a ku baŋ ni n timsa.
english: If you haven't carried me you won't know that I am heavy.
usage: You cannot understand a burden until you experience it

dagbani: A yi che soli ka dari mɔɣu goo yi ti kuhi a a ni labina.
english: If you leave the path and thorns pierce you will return.
usage: Straying leads to suffering that brings you back

dagbani: A yi ʒi so bee zuɣu baŋmi ni a ʒila tiŋa.
english: If you sit on someone's leg know that you sit on the ground.
usage: Relying on others is an unstable foundation

dagbani: A zuɣu yi viɛla a bi mal' li sɔŋdi soli ni.
english: If your head is good you don't put it on the road.
usage: Good fortune should not be carelessly discarded

dagbani: Bɛ yi tabiri a gbini a durila yom.
english: If people support your buttocks you climb quickly.
usage: With support you achieve things faster

dagbani: Bɛ yi zaŋ jɛrigu niŋ tol' ni bɛ ku tooi to jɛrilim yih' o ni.
english: If they put a fool in a mortar they can't pound folly out of him.
usage: Foolishness cannot be beaten out of a person

dagbani: Bia ŋun ka yaa larila o ba turi.
english: The child without strength laughs at his father's insults.
usage: The weak mock others to feel stronger

dagbani: Bin' ŋun bili yila ku kpe voli ni.
english: Whatever has horns cannot enter a hole.
usage: Your nature limits your options

dagbani: Birigu zomiya yɛla jahiya.
english: The deaf and dumb person has become blind; everything is spoiled.
usage: Things have gone from bad to worse

dagbani: Buŋa yi yɛn luhi a a bi nyari o tiba.
english: If a donkey will throw you don't see its ears.
usage: Trouble comes without warning

dagbani: Chɛli ma ka n zaŋ n nuu n-daa n nyaaŋa.
english: Let me use my hand to buy my back.
usage: One cannot fix one's own faults without help

dagbani: Dabili mi na ʒi n-kpalim be ŋun su o yiŋa.
english: A slave does not remain in the house of his master.
usage: Those without belonging have no permanent place

dagbani: Dabisili ti paai wɔbigu ka o ŋuni tutuɣu ni.
english: A day will come when the elephant hides under a bush.
usage: Even the mighty may need to hide

dagbani: Dahin yini tii bi kabiri waliga gbali.
english: One day's race cannot break an antelope's leg.
usage: Achievements take time

dagbani: Dako' viɛliga kpe daa ni ka paɣanim' jɛri suya.
english: A handsome bachelor enters the market and husbands become angry.
usage: Attractive newcomers threaten the established order

dagbani: Dam be duu yɛlli mi beni.
english: There is pito in the room trouble is also there.
usage: Pleasure comes with its own problems

dagbani: Di chani a ʒiʒiinikpee yiŋa waawaayili di yi pa lala o nyee ni ti nyɔni a.
english: Don't go to your neighbour's house too often or he will become weary of you.
usage: Familiarity breeds contempt

dagbani: Di niŋ nira yi doli nira o tɔna gbahirila palɔya.
english: If unlucky man follows lucky man his traps catch only toads.
usage: Luck cannot be transferred by association

dagbani: Di yi ti niŋ ka paɣipuulana yaaŋa biɛra di wuhiya ni bia maa zoorimi maa.
english: If a pregnant woman has backache it shows the baby is growing.
usage: Discomfort can signal progress

dagbani: Din be gariŋga zuɣu ni dina m-be lɛkakuli kpali ni.
english: What is on the night adder's head is also on the axe's backside.
usage: Different things may share hidden similarities

dagbani: Din du zuɣusaa ku biɛla tiŋa.
english: What goes up will not be small on earth.
usage: What rises high will impact below

dagbani: Din nyɛ saɣiri ku lahi leei din yɛn niŋ kasi.
english: What is dirty cannot become clean again.
usage: Some stains are permanent

dagbani: Doo bi nya o to n-yɛli ka di bara ka o ti nya o to n-taɣi zilinli.
english: When a man's opponent is absent he talks proudly; when he sees him he changes.
usage: People boast in absence but moderate face to face

dagbani: Duŋ bi waara.
english: Enmity never dies.
usage: Conflicts can last forever

dagbani: Faralana ka sima faralana ka dima.
english: A poor person has no friend; a poor person has no enemy.
usage: Poverty isolates you

dagbani: Fɔŋbia puli kpuŋ sama yuɣiyuɣi.
english: The town person's stomach is full of debts.
usage: Urban life brings financial obligations

dagbani: Gbɛɣu yi ti laɣim ni jɛɣu bɛ ni yɛli ni bɛ bala.
english: When forehead meets eyebrow they will say they are related.
usage: Close associates are assumed to be related

dagbani: Gbina n-yi chɛ ka doo boli paɣa dooni amaa di nyaaŋa ŋun yɛla bi lan tɛhir' o.
english: The buttocks made a man invite a woman to sleep but he doesn't care for her needs.
usage: Physical desire may not ensure lasting care

dagbani: Gbinzɛri yi chaŋ Naawuni sani o bi saɣira.
english: If a cripple goes to God he is not refused.
usage: God does not discriminate

dagbani: Gbulli ni kuni ka bɛ booni li la noli.
english: When the penis enters they call it the mouth.
usage: Things are named differently than described

dagbani: Gbulli yi ti yi o ni kuli bɔri la di zaashee.
english: When the penis comes out it seeks its place.
usage: Everything seeks its proper place

dagbani: Gbulli yi yi na o dɔɣirila o ma.
english: When the penis comes out it gives birth to its mother.
usage: The child's birth redefines the mother

dagbani: Gbulli yi yi na o doli la o ma.
english: When the penis comes out it follows its mother.
usage: The child follows mother's guidance

dagbani: Gbulli yi yi na o laɣinda la o ma.
english: When the penis comes out it gathers with its mother.
usage: New life is connected to its origin

dagbani: Gbulli yi yi na o mi o ma.
english: When the penis comes out it knows its mother.
usage: The child instinctively knows its mother

dagbani: Gbulli yi yi na o tibgiri o ma.
english: When the penis comes out it honours its mother.
usage: The child honours its mother

dagbani: Gbulli yi yi na o yuri o ma.
english: When the penis comes out it loves its mother.
usage: The child's first love is for mother

dagbani: Guli yuurila bɛn tibigi li noli ni.
english: Kola nuts last longer in the mouth of those who value them.
usage: Things are better preserved by those who value them

dagbani: Jɛrigu n-zabiri sinsabiga zuɣu ka di na pumda di na bi woli.
english: The fool quarrels over a tree that hasn't fruited.
usage: Don't count chickens before they hatch

dagbani: Jɛrigu ʒi gbɛligu naɣla zukɔɣili.
english: The fool doesn't know you warn him unless you knock his head.
usage: A fool needs direct correction

dagbani: Kɔbiliga ni zimbila bi mi ni teeku beni.
english: The small fish in the well doesn't know there is an ocean.
usage: Perspective is limited by one's small world

dagbani: Kpakpili ʒini n-tabili tia ku teei nabili.
english: A tortoise sits against a tree but cannot stretch its legs.
usage: Reliance on others limits your freedom

dagbani: Lari pala yurilim.
english: Laughter is not love.
usage: Friendliness is not affection

dagbani: M baɣabaɣa n-daan tarima m mini nabia bi tɔri woli.
english: I'm a commoner I don't play draughts with a prince.
usage: Know your place avoid risky entanglements

dagbani: Mali tamaha gbe kum ka bɛn' daŋ wolima pulaa lo.
english: Those who hope go hungry; those who act are satisfied.
usage: Action beats passive hope

dagbani: Naa kpi
english: The chief has died
usage: Used when something has ended with no hope of return

dagbani: Naawuni yi bi nam a waribara a yi da muɣuri bahi ni kpuɣi li.
english: If God didn't make you a rider dogs will carry your boots.
usage: Possessions are useless without proper status

dagbani: Nakɔha bi kuli zoo ŋun ni kuli?
english: Will the fly go home before the butcher?
usage: Dependents cannot outlive providers

dagbani: Nira yi vuɣiri tigbura ti ni nya o ni yɛn kɔri shɛm.
english: If a person uproots stumps we see how he farms.
usage: Character shows in handling difficult tasks

dagbani: Noo yi ti doni n-zahim zahinli ka o wari Damba to! -- ʒiɛduɣu m-boon' o.
english: When the fowl dreams of dancing Damba the pot is calling it.
usage: Aspirations may lead to downfall

dagbani: Ŋun' vaai n-ʒi ŋun' ka yɛla doli.
english: One who thinks too much of himself has problems.
usage: Conceit leads to difficulties

dagbani: Ŋun zɔri kuuli n-niŋdi kaasa.
english: The one who ran away says "Whew!"
usage: Relief after escape

dagbani: Ŋun ʒia bi lɔri ŋun chani gbaɣa.
english: The one who doesn't travel doesn't tie loads for the traveler.
usage: Without experience you cannot assist the experienced

dagbani: Sana ku tooi baŋ tiŋ' gbinni.
english: A stranger cannot know a village's secrets.
usage: Outsiders cannot understand inner workings

dagbani: Takahi bi naara naɣla achiama.
english: Problems never end until Judgement Day.
usage: Troubles are permanent

dagbani: Tarim' mila o zaashee.
english: A poor person knows his place.
usage: Even the humble know their position

dagbani: Teeŋa zuu pala kpɛma.
english: A bushy beard does not make an elder.
usage: Appearances don't confer wisdom

dagbani: A yi bɔri ni bɛ bol' a tayiɣa a bi baɣisiri zimsim ni yuŋ.
english: If you don't want to be called a thief you do not sneak about in the dark at night.
usage: Avoid suspicious behaviour if you want a good reputation.

dagbani: A yi bi mi di piribu di ka vi ni a bɔhi ka bɛ wuhi a.
english: If you don't know how to put it on don't be embarrassed to ask someone to show you.
usage: It is not shameful to seek knowledge.

dagbani: A yi chaŋ di gbini di mali la tiyɔɣ' titali di zuɣusaa zaŋ n-ti siɣi di gbinni ka kuɣ' titali do di gbinni.
english: If you go to the bottom of a hollow baobab it has a big hole from top to bottom and there is a big stone lying at the bottom.
usage: Things are often more complex than they appear on the surface.

dagbani: A yi je takahi a bi nyɛri bindi sɔkpaŋa.
english: If you hate to be unpopular you do not defecate on the roadside.
usage: If you can't handle criticism don't do things that attract it.

dagbani: A yi je yɛtɔɣa yoli a bi nyɛri bindi sokpaŋa.
english: If you don't like people criticising you do not defecate on the roadside.
usage: To avoid criticism avoid doing things that invite it.

dagbani: A yi kuli wumdi so yɛla so m-be shɛli n-yɛri a gba yɛla.
english: If you are listening to someone talking about another person someone is somewhere else talking about you.
usage: Gossip is a two-way street.

dagbani: A yi laɣi jɛŋkuno zɔri a ni bɔhim nimkaha ŋubbu.
english: If you want a cat as your friend you will learn to eat raw meat.
usage: You adopt the habits of those you associate with.

dagbani: A yi mini jɛrigu chaŋ mɔɣu ni ka o ti ku n-ti a bimbee a mi yi ti ku nyin' tarimi o bin' zuɣu.
english: If you go hunting with a fool and he kills an animal and gives you the lower leg when you kill one you give him the head.
usage: Deal with people as they deal with you; reciprocity.

dagbani: A yi nya kɔbili ka di do tampuli zuɣu nyin' baŋmi ni dahinshɛli kpam ni yalim ni ka di yina.
english: If you see a bone on the refuse dump know that it once had fat and salt on it.
usage: Everything of value once had its time of glory.

dagbani: A yi tim bia dari ni ka o chaŋ nti ʒe tua gbinni yɛlimi o ni o chɛla dama tua ka dari.
english: If you send a child for firewood and she stands under a baobab tree tell her to stop because baobab is not firewood.
usage: Give clear instructions and ensure they are understood.

dagbani: A yi tu jaŋa ni o nin-lɛɣira di yaɣi diɛma.
english: If you insult the monkey for having deep-set eyes it has gone beyond joking.
usage: Some insults cross the line and are no longer a joke.

dagbani: A yi yɛli ni a ku biɛli dabili yɔɣuni a lee n-ti biɛhi o bindi naba.
english: If you say you will not accompany a slave to the toilet you will trace the footprints of his faeces.
usage: Refusing small help leads to greater inconvenience later.

dagbani: A yi yɛli yɛlimaŋli ka gbe kum di so ni a ŋma ʒiri ka di tiɣi.
english: It is better to tell the truth and go to bed hungry than to tell lies and eat until full.
usage: Honesty and integrity are more valuable than material gain.

dagbani: A yi yɛn yiɣi a zahindila sagbana ka naanyi yiɣi.
english: If you are going to fly you should measure the sky before you fly.
usage: Plan ahead and assess the risks before undertaking a big task.

dagbani: A yi zaŋ kunduŋ gbaŋ she timpani di na kuli kumdila kunduŋ kumsi.
english: If you use a hyena's skin to cover a drum it will still sound like a hyena.
usage: True nature cannot be hidden; it will always show itself.

dagbani: A yi ʒiri malifa tim a ni lan nyurila taba?
english: If you are carrying gunpowder will you also be smoking a pipe?
usage: Do not engage in risky behaviour alongside dangerous activities.

dagbani: A zo simo n-nyɛ ŋun yɛn yɛli a a tuumbiɛri.
english: Your true friend is the one who will tell you your faults.
usage: True friendship includes honest correction.

dagbani: Alibarika tibila yi kuli pɔri kabo di ni lɛbigi yɔɣu.
english: No matter how small a blessed tree is it will become a forest.
usage: Great things can grow from small blessed beginnings.

dagbani: Alizanda soli bili mɔri wuntizɔriba n-kani.
english: If heaven's path is overgrown with grass there are no good persons.
usage: A lack of righteous people leads to neglect of the right path.

dagbani: Baa ŋun je tihigibu niŋdila yum o kpali ni.
english: The dog that does not want to prosper develops a sore on its head.
usage: Those who resist improvement will suffer consequences.

dagbani: Baa yi zɔra a bi mal' o kariti wɔbiri.
english: Even if your dog runs fast you do not chase elephants with it.
usage: Use appropriate tools and people for the task at hand.

dagbani: Baɣibaɣi noli tiligi tapaɣa.
english: The one who apologizes is safe from being slapped.
usage: A sincere apology can prevent further conflict.

dagbani: Bandɔɣu yi mali kɔbiri o zuli ni bɛ bi boon' o jɛŋkuno.
english: Even if a lizard has hair on its tail they do not call it a cat.
usage: Changing appearance does not change your true identity.

dagbani: Bɛn daŋ wolima ni tiligi saa.
english: Those who start struggling early will be saved from the rain.
usage: Early effort and preparation lead to success.

dagbani: Bɛn do yɛliga bi nya Naawuni balle bɛ m-pili bɛ puya zuɣu n-ni nya?
english: Those who lie on their backs cannot see God how much more those on their stomachs?
usage: One's attitude affects their spiritual perception.

dagbani: Biɛɣu kam wahala saɣiri li mi.
english: Sufficient unto the day is the evil thereof.
usage: Do not worry about tomorrow's troubles.

dagbani: Bilikɔɣino gbinni ku yaɣisi baŋbu.
english: The secrets of a hypocrite are quickly known.
usage: Hypocrisy is soon exposed.

dagbani: Binnyɛra din bindi tiŋgban' ni ka mali biɛlim mini tihi din wala mali kpila balibu kam zaa bilimiya.
english: Let the land produce vegetation: seed-bearing plants and trees that bear fruit.
usage: Creation follows divine order and purpose.

dagbani: Bua yino gbali bilisi ka pii.
english: People turn over the thighs of a slaughtered goat before selecting one.
usage: Careful examination precedes wise choice.

dagbani: Buɣ' tikpira ku yɛli wum.
english: A deaf god will not hear someone begging.
usage: Appealing to those who cannot hear is futile.

dagbani: Buɣili yi kum buɣi' tindana n-kum ti o.
english: If a 'god' cries its priest makes it cry.
usage: Those with vested interests may manipulate situations.

dagbani: Bulaa tulaale nyɛla o paɣa dulim.
english: The male goat's perfume is the urine of its wife.
usage: What is prized by one may be common to others.

dagbani: Bulaa yi ti kpɛ kpatuɣa ni o lɔrigiri la o nyɔɣu.
english: When a male goat enters a thicket he unties his chest.
usage: One adapts when entering a difficult situation.

dagbani: Buna bee buhi yi diri laɣinda bɛ yi ti kari ba bɛ zɔri la taba.
english: When sheep or goats eat together and are chased they run together.
usage: Unity in good times should mean unity in trouble.

dagbani: Chaŋ tiŋ' shɛli di yi pa di ni ka a kuli niŋdi nambɔɣu.
english: If you are going to a town do acts of kindness there.
usage: Everywhere you go you should do good.

dagbani: Di bi tu ni o nyɛla ŋun nyuri dam kuuna bee vubɔra.
english: It is not right that a leader should be a drunkard or rowdy.
usage: Leaders must be of good character.

dagbani: Di zaŋdi bɛ yɛliya tumdi tuma faashee a yulila di puuni ka di tuhi.
english: Do not act on rumors; investigate the case and judge justly.
usage: Verify information before taking action.

dagbani: Doo ni paɣa yi laɣim bɛ ni tooi duɣi ʒɛri.
english: If a man and a woman come together they can cook soup.
usage: Cooperation between spouses leads to good things.

dagbani: Kpaŋ chamiya pumpɔŋɔ ka o na yoli paa' na.
english: Hurry now for he has just arrived in the town.
usage: Seize a timely opportunity.

dagbani: Naɣ' so ŋun ka zuli Naawuni n-kariti o zɔhi.
english: The one who has no tail it is God who drives away the flies.
usage: Have faith when you have no one to rely on.

dagbani: Naɣila a doli HIV taɣibu saɣisigu soya.
english: Unless you follow the recommendations for AIDS protection.
usage: Following expert advice is essential for safety.
